title: Equivalent Fraction vs Simplest Form
summary_text: Equivalent fractions name the same value, while simplest form asks for the most reduced name for that value.

left_profile.definition: Fractions that represent the same value using different numerators and denominators.

right_profile.definition: The most reduced name for a fraction after dividing by the greatest common factor.

shared_traits: Both can appear inside the same worked example about one fraction value.
Both are used when learners compare 2/4 with 1/2.
decisive_differences: Equivalent fractions focus on same value across different names.
Simplest form focuses on the single reduced name.
common_confusions: Learners think simplest form means any matching fraction.
Learners see the same example and miss the difference in purpose.

concept_attributes.attribute_value: Different fraction names can still represent the same value.
Divide numerator and denominator by the greatest common factor.

comparison_rows.left_value: Same value with a different fraction name
comparison_rows.right_value: Most reduced version of the fraction
comparison_rows.decisive_clue: Equivalent means same value; simplest form means reduced answer.
comparison_rows.teaching_note: Students often merge these because the same numbers can appear in both.

comparison_rows.left_value: Generate or identify another matching fraction
comparison_rows.right_value: Reduce the fraction by its greatest common factor
comparison_rows.overlap_note: Both can appear in one worked example using 2/4 and 1/2.
comparison_rows.decisive_clue: Simplest form asks for one final reduced name.
comparison_rows.teaching_note: Ask what the question is requesting, not just what values are shown.

prompt_payload.caption: Two shaded fraction bars showing the same amount.

diagram_assets.decisive_visual_clue: The same shaded amount appears with two different fraction names.
diagram_assets.trap_potential: Learners may think any matching diagram already means simplest form.

mode_items.source_atom_text: Represents the same value using a different numerator and denominator.
mode_items.prompt_text: Represents the same value using a different numerator and denominator.

explanation_bundle.why_correct: Equivalent fractions are different names for the same amount.
explanation_bundle.why_other_wrong: Simplest form asks for the reduced final name, not just another matching fraction.
explanation_bundle.missed_clue: same value using a different numerator and denominator

mode_items.source_atom_text: Writes the fraction after dividing by the greatest common factor.
mode_items.prompt_text: Writes the fraction after dividing by the greatest common factor.

explanation_bundle.why_correct: Simplest form is produced by dividing by the greatest common factor.
explanation_bundle.why_other_wrong: Equivalent fractions can be matching names, but they do not require the most reduced answer.

mode_items.source_atom_text: 2/4 and 1/2 belong together as an example pair.
mode_items.prompt_text: 2/4 and 1/2 belong together as an example pair.

explanation_bundle.why_correct: Those two fractions are equivalent because they name the same value.
explanation_bundle.why_other_wrong: Simplest form would ask which single fraction is the reduced answer.

explanation_bundle.why_correct: 1/2 is the final reduced form of 2/4.
explanation_bundle.why_other_wrong: Equivalent fractions could include 3/6, 4/8, or 1/2, but simplest form asks for the reduced answer.

mode_items.source_atom_text: Can appear in the same worked example about 2/4 and 1/2.
mode_items.prompt_text: Both ideas can appear in the same worked example about 2/4 and 1/2.

explanation_bundle.why_correct: The same example can teach both ideas.
explanation_bundle.why_other_wrong: The difference is the question being asked about the example.

mode_items.prompt_text: Which statement best describes Equivalent Fraction rather than Simplest Form?

answer_options.label: Same value with a different fraction name
Most reduced version of the fraction
Both always mean the reduced answer

mode_items.correct_choice_label: Same value with a different fraction name

explanation_bundle.why_correct: Equivalent fractions keep the value the same while changing the name.
explanation_bundle.why_other_wrong: Simplest form is specifically the reduced final name.

mode_items.source_atom_text: Writes the fraction after dividing by the greatest common factor.

explanation_bundle.why_correct: Reducing by the greatest common factor points to simplest form.
explanation_bundle.why_other_wrong: Equivalent fraction is broader and not limited to the reduced answer.

mode_items.prompt_text: Identify the idea before all clues are revealed.

explanation_bundle.why_correct: The decisive clue is the reduced final answer.
explanation_bundle.why_other_wrong: Equivalent fractions can match the value but do not require the reduced form.

atoms.atom_text: Represents the same value using a different numerator and denominator.
atoms.explanation_text: Equivalent fractions are different names for the same amount.
atoms.trap_angle: same value does not automatically mean reduced form

review_payload.why_correct: Equivalent fractions keep the value the same across different names.
review_payload.why_other_wrong: Simplest form is one reduced name, not every matching name.
review_payload.missed_clue: same value using a different numerator and denominator

atoms.atom_text: Writes the fraction after dividing by the greatest common factor.
atoms.explanation_text: Simplest form is the most reduced version of a fraction.

review_payload.why_correct: Simplest form is produced by reducing with the greatest common factor.
review_payload.why_other_wrong: Equivalent fractions may match the value without being the reduced answer.

atoms.atom_text: 2/4 and 1/2 belong together as an example pair.
atoms.explanation_text: Those two fractions are equivalent because they represent the same value.

review_payload.why_correct: The pair shows two names for the same value.
review_payload.why_other_wrong: Simplest form would ask which single name is reduced.

atoms.explanation_text: Simplest form asks for the reduced version, not just another matching fraction.

review_payload.why_correct: 1/2 is the reduced final form of 2/4.
review_payload.why_other_wrong: Equivalent fractions could include many matching names, not only the reduced one.

atoms.atom_text: Can appear in the same worked example about 2/4 and 1/2.
atoms.explanation_text: The same example can teach both ideas, but each idea answers a different question.

review_payload.why_correct: Both ideas can live inside the same example.
review_payload.why_other_wrong: What changes is the purpose of the question.

atoms.atom_text: Add the numerator and denominator together to get the answer.
atoms.explanation_text: Adding the numerator and denominator does not define equivalent fractions or simplest form.

review_payload.why_correct: That shortcut does not belong to either idea.
review_payload.why_other_wrong: Neither equivalent fractions nor simplest form are found by adding top and bottom.
